label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the order of the prices of these five fruits: kiwis, pears, peaches, loquats, and apples. The question gives me some inequalities and asks which one is the second-cheapest. The options are A to E. Let me start by listing out all the given information: 1. Loquats are more expensive than kiwis. So, loquats > kiwis. 2. Pears are more expensive than peaches. So, pears > peaches. 3. Apples are more expensive than loquats. So, apples > loquats. 4. Apples are less expensive than peaches. So, apples < peaches. Now I need to arrange all these in order from cheapest to most expensive. Let me try to chain these inequalities together. Let me write them as: From 1: loquats > kiwis → kiwis < loquats From 2: pears > peaches → peaches < pears From 3: apples > loquats → loquats < apples From 4: apples < peaches → apples
